title: Fix admin panel blog post display and editing

requirements.summary: Fix the admin panel (AdminPostsPage) so that all blog posts are fetched and displayed correctly in the posts list table, including posts created via the Create Blog Post form
acceptanceCriteria: All blog posts (published and draft) appear in the admin panel list when navigating to the admin page
Each post row shows the post title, category, publish status, and action buttons (edit, delete, toggle publish)
The edit button navigates to the EditBlogPostPage for the selected post
The delete button triggers a confirmation dialog and removes the post on confirmation
The toggle publish button correctly switches a post between published and draft states
Loading and empty states are handled gracefully with appropriate messages

file_operations.description: Update the AdminPostsPage component to properly fetch and display all blog posts (including drafts) using the useGetAllVisiblePosts hook. Ensure the posts list table shows title, category, publish status (with visual distinction for drafts via a badge), and action buttons (edit, delete, toggle publish). Wire the edit button to navigate to EditBlogPostPage with the post ID. Implement a confirmation dialog for the delete action. Ensure the toggle publish button correctly updates the post's published state. Add proper loading spinner and empty state messaging when no posts exist. Verify that newly created posts from the Create Blog Post form appear in the list immediately after creation.

requirements.summary: Ensure the admin panel uses the correct React Query hook that fetches ALL blog posts (including unpublished drafts), not just published posts, so admins can manage all content
acceptanceCriteria: AdminPostsPage calls the hook that retrieves all visible posts (useGetAllPosts or equivalent), not the published-only hook
Draft posts are visible in the admin list and are visually distinguished from published posts (e.g., a 'Draft' badge)

file_operations.description: Review and ensure the useGetAllVisiblePosts hook (or equivalent) correctly calls the backend's getAllPosts method to fetch all blog posts regardless of publish status. Verify the hook is properly exported and available for use in AdminPostsPage. If the hook does not exist or is incorrectly named, add or rename it to useGetAllVisiblePosts for clarity. Ensure it uses the actor's getAllPosts method from the backend interface.

Replace any usage of useGetPublishedPosts with useGetAllVisiblePosts (or the correct hook that fetches all posts) to ensure draft posts are included in the admin panel list. Add visual distinction for draft posts by rendering a 'Draft' badge or label next to unpublished posts in the table rows. Ensure the component correctly handles the data structure returned by the all-posts hook.
